extern type Number;
extern type Bool;
cap Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self }
extern fn js_sub(a: Number, b: Number): Number;
extern fn js_is_zero(n: Number): Bool;
impl Number: Sub { fn sub(a: Self, b: Self): Self = resume(js_sub(a, b)) }

fn is_even(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 1 } else { is_odd(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn is_odd(n: Number): Number = if js_is_zero(n) { 0 } else { is_even(Sub.sub(n, 1)) }
fn main(): Number = is_even(4)

===EXPECT===
# Mutual recursion: `is_even` calls `is_odd` and vice versa. SCC-level
# dep-free analysis groups {is_even, is_odd} into a single SCC, classifies
# the whole SCC DepFree, and commits perform_resolution for both bodies
# atomically.
#
# Both fns are cloned (`is_even__lto` / `is_odd__lto`). `main` calls
# `is_even__lto` directly. Inside each clone, calls to the other fn are
# redirected to the other clone, so the mutual recursion stays inside the
# non-CPS clone pair. The original CPS `is_even` / `is_odd` become
# unreachable and are DCE'd.
is_even__lto
is_odd__lto
js_sub(a, b)
return is_odd__lto
return is_even__lto
!function is_even(__caps
!function is_odd(__caps
!(__caps, 4, __k)
